goal: Bring internal development contour to full operational readiness, including reliable hourly Telegram reports and auditor sync.
operator_problem: Hourly Telegram reports are still not arriving. Internal contour works as file-based governed contour, but needs runtime hardening and full proof loops.
principles: Do not use audit_mailbox as general role-bus. Use it only for external auditor to internal auditor sync and reverse direction.
Internal tasks still use internal_contour role-bus.
Claude primary can be selected only with runtime proof; otherwise reserve route is valid and explicit.
Telegram hourly report is not production-ready until delivery_confirmed=true.

workstreams.name: Telegram hourly delivery repair
workstreams.objective: Make curator-hourly-report.yml actually deliver hourly reports and persist delivery proof.
tasks: Verify TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN and TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID presence without printing secrets
Check workflow triggers: schedule and manual dispatch
Record HTTP status and Telegram API response safely
Persist governance/state/curator_hourly_report_state.json with telegram_delivery=sent or exact blocker
acceptance: Operator receives canonical hourly report in Telegram

workstreams.name: Claude primary runtime proof
workstreams.objective: If Claude is currently available, prove it with Claude-authored runtime artifact and select primary where appropriate.
tasks: If claude_runtime_proven=true, update provider route to primary_proven
If not, keep reserve route with reason
acceptance: Claude primary selected only if runtime proof exists
Provider route states selected_provider and reserve_used clearly

workstreams.name: Internal auditor sync
workstreams.objective: Use audit_mailbox only for external auditor ↔ internal auditor agreement on roadmap and readiness.
tasks: External auditor sends this plan to audit_mailbox/external_auditor_to_internal_auditor
Internal auditor creates final verdict in internal_contour/auditor/reports
acceptance: Mailbox contains both request and response

workstreams.name: Role-bus execution hardening
workstreams.objective: Ensure every future task has curator, orchestrator, analyst, internal auditor, executor, selftest, final report artifacts.
tasks: Add missing router if mailbox response is not automatically imported to internal auditor inbox
Ensure final reports show execution_type and provider proof

workstreams.name: Final readiness acceptance
workstreams.objective: Close development only after all required checks and operational watches are resolved or explicitly classified.
tasks: Run controls C1-C7 plus Telegram delivery C8
Mark blockers null only with evidence
acceptance: Final readiness status production_ready or production_ready_with_named_watch

required_controls: C5 Claude runtime proof or explicit reserve route pass
questions_for_internal_auditor: Do you approve this workstream split W1-W5?
Should Telegram delivery C6 be hard blocker for production readiness? Recommended: yes for hourly-report readiness.
If Claude runtime proof passes, should internal auditor/executor primary provider switch from reserve to Claude primary for next real implementation?
Do you require any additional checks before final readiness acceptance?
